In the rules of marriage eligibility, what is the term 'Chhadvith'?
It refers to the sixteen specific ancestral points (sixth generation 
markers) used to check for relatedness.
21194
If a bride's 'Chhadvith' ancestor appears in the groom's paternal line, 
is the marriage allowed?
No, it is prohibited.
21195
Under what condition is a 'Chhadvith' ancestor appearing in the 
groom's lineage permitted for marriage?
If the ancestor appears only in the groom's maternal line (after the 
5th generation).
21196
Which Maithil scholar was awarded the title 'Panji Shastra 
Martanda' by Maharaja Kameshwar Singh?
Modanand Jha.
21197
What material was used for the oldest extant Panji records, such as 
those from the $17$th century?
Palm leaves (Talpatra).
21198
Why is the Maithil Panji system considered 'unparalleled' in the 
world according to the source?
Because it contains a continuous and comprehensive genealogical 
web of a whole community spanning over $1000$ years.
21199
What does 'Ansh' mean in the context of the Panji records?
The numerical grade or status point assigned to a specific lineage 
within a hierarchy.
21200
Which Gotra includes the Mools of Khardore, Khauare, and 
Sodarpurie?
Shandilya.
21201
During which historical period was the Panji system formally 
organised in Mithila?
The late medieval period, specifically the early 14th century (c. 
1326 AD).
21202
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal initiation and 
patronage of the Panji records?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
21203
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Uteh' represent?
A genealogical introduction spanning 32 ancestral lines (paternal 
and maternal) required for marriage eligibility.
21204
According to the marriage rules in the Panji system, how many 
paternal generations must be avoided for a marriage to be valid?
Seven generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21205
For Shrotriya Brahmins, how many maternal generations must be 
avoided for marriage eligibility?
Five generations.
21206
What are the four categories into which Maithil Brahmins were 
divided under the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddha, and Jaybar.
21207
What happened to individuals categorized as 'Jaybar' in the Panji 
system?
They were those who were not formally recorded in the 
genealogical registers (Panjis).
21208
How many primary Gotras are traditionally recognized among 
Maithil Brahmins according to the source material?
Twenty (20) Gotras.
21209
Name the two major Vedic branches (Shakhas) followed by Maithil 
Brahmins.
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
21210
Maithil Brahmins following the Yajurveda branch are commonly 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
21211
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda branch are commonly 
referred to as _____.
Chhandog
21212
What is the primary difference in marriage rituals between 
Chhandog and Vajasaneyi Brahmins regarding timing?
Chhandog marriages include a division into 'Purva' and 'Uttar' (post-
sunset) rites, whereas Vajasaneyi rites have no such time restriction.
21213
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Beeji Purush' signify?
The earliest known common ancestor or founder of a specific 
lineage (Mool).
21214
What triggered the need for formalised Panji records in the 14th 
century?
The marriage of scholar Pandit Harinath Upadhyay to his first 
cousin due to a lack of genealogical knowledge.
21215
What is the specific year (in the Shaka era) associated with the 
establishment of the Panji Prabandh?
Shaka 1248 (corresponding to 1326 AD).
21216
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Aswajana Patra)?
A formal certificate of marriage eligibility issued by a Panjikar 
confirming that the bride and groom are not prohibited relatives.
21217
Who was the first official Panjikar (registrar) appointed for Maithil 
Brahmins by King Harisimhadeva?
Saduropadhyaya Gunakar Jha.
21218
Who was the official Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community by King Harisimhadeva?
Shankardatta
21219
Which Panjikar was appointed for the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community during the reign of Harisimhadeva?
Vijayadatta
21220
What is the 'Mool' of a person in the Panji system?
The ancestral village or place of origin of their lineage.
21221
Define 'Pravara' in the context of Maithil Brahmin genealogy.
A lineage of Rishi ancestors associated with a Gotra who were 
composers of Vedic hymns.
21222
Which three Gotras are categorized as 'Pancha-Pravara' (having five 
ancestral Rishis)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though the text also lists Gargya).
21223
Identify the three Rishi ancestors associated with the Shandilya 
Gotra.
Shandilya, Asita, and Devala.
21224
Identify the three Rishi ancestors associated with the Kashyap Gotra. Kashyap, Avatsar, and Naidhruva.
21225
In the Panji system, what are 'Moolgrams'?
The original villages from which various branches of a lineage 
dispersed.
21226
How many primary 'Mool' (roots) were initially identified for the 
167 village-based groups?
Thirty-four (34), which later expanded to thirty-five (35) with the 
inclusion of the Khanama branch.
21227
What does 'Chhadwith' refer to in marriage eligibility checks?
The determination that a person is at least in the 'sixth place' 
(generation) of distance from a common ancestor to avoid 
prohibited sapinda relations.
21228
How many specific 'Chhadwith' points (ancestral relations) are 
checked by a Panjikar for a bride?
Sixteen (16).
21229
What is the 'Dooshan Panji'?
A record of deviations, 'defects', or breaches in social/caste norms 
within a lineage.
21230
Under which ruler was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
introduced to further classify the community into Shrotriya and 
other ranks?
Maharaja Madhava Singh (c. 1760 AD).
21231
The Gotra of Karna Kayasthas in Mithila is exclusively _____.
Kashyap
21232
Why are Panji records dried during the month when the Sun enters 
Leo (August/September)?
Because the intense heat of that season is most effective for 
preserving the palm leaves from damp and pests.
21233
What was the significance of Kumarila Bhattaâ€™s 'Samuha 
Lekhya' mentioned in the 7th century?
It is the earliest reference to collective written records maintained 
by noble families to preserve their lineage history.
21234
The Panji system classifies Brahmin villages into three grades of 
'Ayan-Shrestha' (Excellent Roots). Name the first grade.
First grade includes villages like Khardaur, Khauar, Budhabal, 
Madariya, and Darihar.
21235
In 'Tri-pravara', how many Vedic Rishi ancestors are cited?
Three.
21236
In 'Pancha-pravara', how many Vedic Rishi ancestors are cited?
Five.
21237
Define 'Sapinda' as used in the source material for marriage 
restrictions.
Blood relatives within prohibited degrees of kinship, typically 
determined through paternal and maternal lineage counts.
21238
Who was the Panjikar who received the 'Panji Shastra Martand' title 
from the Maithili Academy?
Modanand Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21239
The Panji records identify lineages of the famous poet Vidyapati. 
How many generations are recorded between him and his ancestor 
Tirthayibhatta?
Seven generations.
21240
What does the 'Ghataka' or 'Panjikar' check before issuing a 
marriage permit?
They verify the 32 mools (ancestral lines) of both parties to ensure 
no overlap within prohibited degrees.
21241
According to the source, why did Panji records eventually stop 
being maintained for Kshatriyas (Rajputs)?
Over time, the practice fell into disuse, and they began relying on 
avoiding the family village (Gram) as a simple marriage rule instead.
21242
Which Gotra includes Rishis like Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar?
Parashar Gotra.
21243
Which Gotra includes Rishis like Bharadwaj, Angiras, and 
Barhaspatya?
Bharadwaj Gotra.
21244
Which Gotra includes Rishis like Vishwamitra, Atri, and 
Jamadagni?
Kaushik Gotra.
21245
What is a 'Mool-Gotra Panji'?
A register that correlates ancestral roots (Mools) with their 
corresponding Gotras.
21246
The first Chhadwith point for a bride is the _____.
Paternal Great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
21247
The ninth Chhadwith point for a bride is the _____.
Maternal Great-great-grandfather (Matamaha Prapitamaha).
21248
What is the social consequence mentioned for 'Swajana' marriages 
(marrying prohibited relatives)?
The offspring were traditionally considered 'Chandal' and excluded 
from the high-caste Aryan social fold.
21249
Which group of Maithil Brahmins is referred to as 'Pachhwari Paat'? The non-Shrotriya Brahmins, including Yogya and Panjibaddha 
ranks.
21250
How many total Panji registers exist for the 'Yogya' and 
'Panjibaddha' categories?
One hundred and fifty-three (153).
21251
According to the source, what is the role of a Panjikar towards the 
future generation?
To prepare and train students/disciples to ensure the continuity of 
genealogical expertise and record-keeping.
21252
The term used for the 'Genome Mapping' of Mithila in the title 
refers to the _____.
Panji Prabandh
21253
In the Panji system, individuals who share the same Gotra but have 
different Mools are _____ to marry.
Prohibited (due to Gotra-exogamy rules).
21254
What is recorded in the 'Patra Panji'?
Details regarding the specific lineages and villages associated with 
current residents.
21255
Which Maithil Brahmin ancestor is cited as the founder of the 
'Mandir' Mool of the Kashyap Gotra?
Tirthayibhatta
21256
Identify the Gotra associated with the Rishi 'Gautama'.
Gautama Gotra.
21257
Identify the Gotra associated with the Rishis 'Katyayana', 'Vishnu', 
and 'Angiras'.
Katyayana Gotra.
21258
What is the 'Upanayana' rite difference for Samavedi (Chhandog) 
Brahmins?
It involves four specific Sanskaras: Namakarana, Chudakarana, 
Upanayana, and Samavartana.
21259
What additional rite is included in the Upanayana of Yajurvedi 
(Vajasaneyi) Brahmins compared to Samavedis?
Vedarambha (the start of Vedic study).
21260
How did the Panjikar 'Modanand Jha' receive his records?
He was the 18th generation descendant of the original Panjikar 
Gunakar Jha.
21261
What is the primary material traditionally used for writing Panji 
records?
Palm leaves (Talpatra).
21262
Which Sanskrit verse is taught to Panji students to warn about book 
preservation?
$Jalat Raksha Tailat Raksha Raksha Shithila Bandhanat$ (Protect 
from water, oil, and loose binding).
21263
What does the 'Adhikara' (Right) investigation determine?
Whether a specific bride and groom have the legal and religious 
right to marry based on their ancestry.
21264
In the classification of Panji, what is 'Asawajanyapatra'?
A specific type of Siddhanta or clearance document.
21265
Which kingâ€™s arrival in Mithila is marked by the Shaka year 
1019 in the introductory verses?
Nanyadeva
21266
What was the rank of the first official Panjikar, Gunakar Jha?
Saduropadhyaya
21267
How many Rajput clans are mentioned in the 'Varna Ratnakara' in 
relation to Mithila's genealogy?
Seventy-two (72) clans, with sixty-four (64) specifically described.
21268
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins include Maithil, Saraswat, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, and _____.
Utkal
21269
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins are separated from the 'Pancha 
Gauda' by which geographical feature?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
21270
What is the significance of the 35th Mool, the 'Khanama' branch of 
the Phandah Mool?
It was added in later periods as a branch that attained high social 
status.
21271
Identify the Gotra associated with 'Vishnuvriddha', 'Kautipuchha', 
and 'Trasadasyu'.
Vishnuvriddha Gotra.
21272
Identify the Gotra associated with 'Maudgalya', 'Angiras', and 
'Barhaspatya'.
Maudgalya Gotra.
21273
What does the term 'An-Siddhanta' refer to in modern Panji usage?
The request for a Siddhanta Patra to be written in the Devanagari 
script for easier reading by the general public.
21274
Which historical era is used for most dates in the Panji registers?
Shaka Era (Shaka Samvat).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21275
True or False: The Panji system allows for marriage if the maternal 
grandmother's line overlaps within 4 generations.
False (The maternal line typically requires a 5-6 generation gap).
21276
In the lists of 'Excellent Roots' (Ayan-Shrestha), name three villages 
from the second category.
Bambholiban, Parabali-ban, and Kujoli-ban.
21277
What is the 'Beeji' of the 'Ghoghi-Sam' lineage?
Awasthika Supananda.
21278
What is the 'Beeji' of the 'Sodarpur' lineage?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
21279
Who was the 'Beeji' of the 'Simhasram' Mool?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
21280
Which Gotra corresponds to the 'Basaha' Mool?
Gargya Gotra.
21281
In which historical period was the Panji Prabandh system created 
for the purity of Brahmin, Kayastha, and Kshatriya castes?
The Early Medieval period.
21282
What does the term 'Uteh' (or Udehadh) represent in the context of 
Mithila genealogy?
The introduction and record of seven generations of ancestors.
21283
How many total ancestral 'kulas' (lineages) are required for a 
complete 'Uteh' introduction?
Thirty-two.
21284
Into which three main categories were Maithil Brahmins divided 
following the implementation of the Panji system?
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
21285
What is the term for Brahmins who were not formally registered in 
the Panji records?
Jaibar.
21286
How many 'Gotras' are recognized within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the source?
Twenty.
21287
How many 'Mulas' (root villages) are traditionally identified in the 
Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
21288
Out of the 167 total Mulas, how many are considered 'Mukhya' 
(primary) Mulas?
Thirty-four (later expanded to 35).
21289
List the three Gotras that belong to the 'Pancha-pravara' (five 
ancestor) category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though the text specifically lists 
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga as primary examples).
21290
Which historical ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the 
formal establishment of the Panji system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21291
What is the specific year in the Shaka calendar when the Panji 
system was reportedly established?
Saka 1248.
21292
Who was the first person appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Panji records for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
21293
What is the name of the professional genealogist responsible for 
verifying marriage eligibility in Mithila?
Panjikar.
21294
The certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and 
groom are not related is known as _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Asvajana Patra).
21295
What is a 'Beeji Purusha' in Maithil genealogical terms?
The earliest known ancestor or progenitor of a specific lineage.
21296
Which Brahmin category is considered the highest in the social 
hierarchy of Mithila?
Srotriya.
21297
Into how many 'Chaukis' (grades) is the Srotriya classification 
further divided?
Eight.
21298
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' marriage ceremony timing differ from 
the 'Chhandoga' tradition?
Vajasaneyi ceremonies can occur during the day or night, while 
Chhandoga ceremonies must view the stars (Arundhati) after sunset.
21299
The list of 16 specific ancestral relations that must be checked to 
ensure 'non-kinship' is called _____.
Chhadwith.
21300
What is the term for the 'paternal grandfather of the maternal 
grandmother' in the Chhadwith calculation?
The 13th Chhadwith.
21301
Which Maithil King initiated the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) to 
distinguish between higher and lower social ranks?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21302
In the context of Panji, what does the 'Dushana' record track?
Social or lineage blemishes/faults occurring in a family history.
21303
Maithil Brahmins are considered a subset of which broader group 
of North Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda.
21304
How many ancestors are invoked in a 'Tri-pravara' lineage?
Three.
21305
The specific Mula of 'Pandua' belongs to which Gotra?
Kashyapa.
21306
The Mula 'Ere' belongs to which Gotra?
Vatsa.
21307
What is the significance of 'Samuha Lekhya' mentioned by 
Kumarila Bhatta in the 7th century?
It is an early written record of family lineages used to preserve caste 
purity.
21308
Which religious text author is cited as the authority for the rules of 
'Sapinda' marriage restrictions?
Yajnavalkya (and Manu).
21309
In the Panji system, what happens if a person marries a 'Svajana' 
(forbidden relative)?
The offspring are traditionally considered outcasts ('Chandala' 
status) and the family loses social standing.
21310
The 'Ayanthi Shrestha' category refers to which grade of Mulas?
The first or highest grade.
21311
Name one Mula that belongs to the 'Madhyama' (Middle) grade.
Dighwe (or Belaunch, Panchobhe, etc.).
21312
What is the role of 'Asvajana Patra' in a Maithil wedding?
It serves as the formal permission from a Panjikar to proceed with 
the marriage ritual.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21313
Under whose reign was the 'Srotriya' classification solidified 
through the creation of 'Laukik' records?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21314
Which ancient scholar's 'Tantra Varttika' mentions the tradition of 
maintaining written lineage records?
Kumarila Bhatta.
21315
What is the maximum number of generations a Panji record can 
typically trace back for a high-ranking family?
Over 1000 years (approximately 30-40 generations).
21316
The process of verifying the 32 maternal and paternal root lineages 
of a prospective couple is called _____.
Uteh-karana.
21317
Who was the scholar that accidentally married his cousin, 
prompting the formalisation of the Panji system in the 14th century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
21318
What is 'Pachhwari Pa' in the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy?
A classification referring to non-Srotriya Brahmins (lower rank).
21319
What is the specific ancestral village term used when a person's 
ancestors moved to a new location but maintained their root name?
Mula-Grama.
21320
How many 'Chhadwith' positions are derived from the maternal side 
of the bride?
Eight.
21321
What happens to the rank of a 'Srotriya' family if they marry into a 
'Panjibaddh' family?
Their rank decreases (Adhogami).
21322
Which specific ancestor is the '1st Chhadwith'?
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha-Prapitamaha).
21323
Why is the preservation of Tirhuta script essential to the Panji 
system?
Nearly all historical Panji records (Pothis) are written exclusively in 
Tirhuta.
21324
The term for an ancestor who migrates and establishes a new 
branch of a Mula is _____.
Deraderi.
21325
According to the source, how many primary Gotras are considered 
the most prominent/organized in Mithila?
Seven (Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaj, 
Katyayan).
21326
Which document is required for the 'Upanyana' (sacred thread) 
ceremony to determine the correct Pravara recitation?
The Panji record/Uteh.
21327
The 10 branches of Brahmins in India are geographically divided 
into two groups of five called _____ and _____.
Pancha Gauda (North) and Pancha Dravida (South).
21328
What constitutes the '16th Chhadwith' for a bride?
The maternal grandfather of the maternal grandmother (Matri-
Matamaha of the Matamahi).
21329
Which historical figure was the Progenitor (Beeji Purusha) of the 
'Sandilya' Gotra in Mithila?
Sandilya Rishi.
21330
In the context of the Panji system, what is 'Karna Kayastha'?
A Kayastha sub-caste in Mithila that uses a similar genealogical 
system to the Brahmins.
21331
What is the primary Gotra followed by all Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyapa.
21332
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji' in this cultural context?
A genealogical register or chronicle.
21333
Who was the 'Beeji Purusha' of the Mula 'Sarisab'?
Ratnapani.
21334
What is the term for a marriage that violates the 7/5 generation rules 
but is still recorded by Panjikars?
Dushita (Blemished) marriage.
21335
Which historical document provides early information on 72 Rajput 
clans in Mithila?
Varna Ratnakara.
21336
Who authored the 'Varna Ratnakara'?
Jyotirishwar Thakur.
21337
What is the term for a person's 'Maternal Grandfather's Mula'?
Mamamula.
21338
In the Chhadwith check, if a bride is the 6th generation from a 
common ancestor, she is forbidden to marry a groom if he is _____.
Also within the 6th generation on his paternal side.
21339
How does a Panji record help in performing 'Pindadaana' (ancestral 
rites)?
It provides the exact names and lineages of ancestors required for 
the ritual.
21340
What is the significance of the year 2000 in the context of the Panji 
project described?
It was the start of the digital imaging and transliteration project 
(from Tirhuta to Devanagari).
21341
Why is the Panji system compared to 'Genome Mapping' in the 
source title?
Because it tracks hereditary data and biological lineages over 
centuries, similar to genetic mapping.
21342
What is a 'Pa' (or Patra) in the social ranking system of Mithila?
A measure of social or ritual purity/status based on ancestry.
21343
Which Brahmin subgroup has the most extensive 'Laukik' records?
Srotriyas.
21344
What is 'Panji Prabandh' in the context of Mithila culture?
A genealogical record management system used to ensure caste 
purity and marriage eligibility.
21345
In which year was the Panji system formally institutionalised in 
Mithila?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
21346
Which King is credited with the formalisation of the Panji system in 
Mithila?
King Harisimhadev of the Karnat dynasty.
21347
Which three primary social groups were initially covered by the 
Panji Prabandh?
Brahmins, Kayasthas and Kshatriyas.
21348
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
excluded from the maternal side for marriage eligibility?
Five generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21349
How many generations are traditionally excluded from the paternal 
side for a valid marriage under Panji rules?
Seven generations (later modified to six in some contexts).
21350
What is the total number of 'Gotras' identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the text?
20 Gotras.
21351
List the seven most prominent and well-known Gotras in the 
Maithil Brahmin community.
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaj and 
Katyayana.
21352
What is the specific name given to followers of the Madhyandina 
branch of Yajurveda in Mithila?
Vajasaneyi.
21353
What term describes the followers of the Kauthuma branch of 
Samaveda in Mithila?
Chhandogya.
21354
How does the 'Chhandogya' marriage ritual differ from the 
'Vajasaneyi' regarding timing?
Chhandogya marriages must view the star Arundhati after sunset, 
while Vajasaneyi marriages have no such restriction.
21355
Define 'Pravara' in the Maithil Brahmin genealogical system.
The number of ancestors (usually three or five) who were 
composers of Rigvedic hymns.
21356
The Vatsa and Savarna Gotras share the same ancestors; how does 
this affect marriage?
Marriage between members of these two Gotras is prohibited.
21357
In the hierarchy of Maithil Brahmins, who are the 'Srotriyas'?
The highest rank of Brahmins who maintained strict lineage and 
conduct standards.
21358
Who are the 'Panjibaddh' Brahmins?
Brahmins whose lineages were formally recorded in the Panji books 
but were ranked below Srotriyas and Yogyas.
21359
What term is used for those who are not formally recorded in the 
Panji system?
Jaywar.
21360
What does 'Mool' represent in the Panji system?
The ancestral 'root' or original clan identity.
21361
What does 'Moolgram' signify in Mithila genealogy?
The specific original village associated with a particular Mool 
(lineage).
21362
How many total 'Mools' (original lineages) were initially recorded 
for Maithil Brahmins?
167 Mools.
21363
According to the 'Chhattha' (Sixth) rule, what is the prohibited 
distance for marriage from common ancestors?
Neither the bride nor the groom should be within the sixth position 
from any of the sixteen defined ancestors.
21364
Who was Gunakar Jha in the history of Panji Prabandh?
The scholar appointed by King Harisimhadev to lead the first 
formal collection of Brahmin genealogical data.
21365
The 'Pancha Gauda' classification includes which five Brahmin 
groups?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil and Saraswat.
21366
Which mountain range serves as the geographical boundary 
between 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
21367
What is the 'Bijipurush' in a lineage?
The earliest known ancestor or the 'founder' from whom a specific 
branch of a Mool originates.
21368
What does 'Sarpinda' (Sapinda) refer to in marriage law?
Being related through the offering of the same 'Pinda' (funeral 
cake), denoting blood relation.
21369
Why did the Panji system eventually decline among the Kshatriyas 
in Mithila?
Due to the passage of time and the difficulty of maintaining 
exhaustive records for all members.
21370
What is the primary objective of the 'Genome Mapping' 
nomenclature used in the source title?
To frame the traditional Panji system as a pre-modern equivalent of 
genetic tracking.
21371
According to Kumarila Bhatta in 'Tantra Vartika', what is the 
purpose of 'Samuha Lekhya'?
To protect the lineage and memory of ancestors to maintain social 
and ritual purity.
21372
What is the significance of the number 16 in the 'Chhattha' rule?
It refers to the sixteen ancestors (eight maternal and eight paternal) 
whose lineages are checked for overlaps.
21373
The source mentions 'Laukik' in the context of Srotriya Panjis; what 
does it refer to?
The status or category of a family within the Srotriya hierarchy 
based on marriage alliances.
21374
How are Panji books traditionally preserved to prevent damage?
They are protected from water and oil, tied with thick bindings, and 
dried in the sun during the lion sign (August/September).
21375
What is the 'Upanayanam' sacrament difference for Chhandogya 
followers?
It consists of four steps: Namakaran, Chudakaran, Upanayan and 
Samavartan.
21376
Which Gotra is the only one traditionally associated with the Karna 
Kayasthas in the text?
Kashyapa Gotra.
21377
Who was Pandit Harinath Upadhyay, and why is he mentioned 
regarding marriage rules?
A scholar who inadvertently married a prohibited relative, leading 
to the strict formalisation of the Panji system.
21378
Name one of the ancestors involved in the 'Tri-pravara' of the 
Shandilya Gotra.
Asita (or Devala).
21379
Name one of the ancestors involved in the 'Pancha-pravara' of the 
Vatsa Gotra.
Jamadagnya (or Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Apnavana).
21380
What is a 'Mool' without a 'Gram' called, such as in the case of 
'Khannham'?
It is referred to as a branch (Shakha) that later attained the status of 
a Mool.
21381
The text lists 'Ayanthi Shrestha' as the first category of Mools; how 
many are in this group?
13 Mools.
21382
What is the 'Abhista' (goal) of a Panjikar according to the text's 
ethical guidelines?
To serve as a protector of culture rather than merely seeking 
monetary gain.
21383
What is the 'Chaurasi' (84) or 'Chauvala' category in the Srotriya 
Panji?
It refers to the classification of the Srotriya community into specific 
hierarchical 'classes'.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21384
How did regional culture influence Brahmins over time according 
to the text?
Varying climates, languages and diets led Brahmins to divide based 
on their specific region of residence.
21385
What defines the 'Pancha Dravida' group of Brahmins?
The five Brahmin groups residing south of the Vindhyachal 
Mountains: Karnataka, Gurjar, Tailanga, Dravida and Maharashtra.
21386
What was the capital of the ancient Videha kingdom, the precursor 
to Mithila?
Janakpur (implied by Mithila's history).
21387
In Panji records, what information is usually paired with the 
ancestor's name?
Their academic titles, village of residence, and notable deeds or 
faults.
21388
The term 'Siddhanta' is necessary for which event?
The legal and ritual sanctioning of a marriage.
21389
How many Gotras are considered 'Vyavasthit' (systematically 
organised and populous) in Mithila?
Seven Gotras.
21390
What is the meaning of 'Gaurivara' in the context of marriage?
A title given to a groom who marries into a specific high-status 
lineage.
21391
How are 'Sapinda' relationships calculated for the maternal side?
Up to the fifth generation from the mother.
21392
How are 'Sapinda' relationships calculated for the paternal side?
Up to the seventh generation from the father.
21393
Which Sanskrit work by Madhava Singh initiated the 'Shakha 
Panji'?
The text does not name a work but attributes the order to Maharaj 
Madhava Singh in 1760 AD.
21394
What happens if a girl is found to be 'Svajana' (related within 
prohibited degrees) to a boy?
The marriage is considered invalid and any offspring would 
traditionally be socially excluded.
21395
What is the primary role of the 'Bijipurush' in the record books?
To serve as the reference point for calculating the generations of all 
descendants.
21396
Which scholar wrote the 'Smritisara' mentioned in the text?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
21397
The phrase 'Jalad Raksha, Tailad Raksha' is a mnemonic for what?
Protecting Panji manuscripts from water and oil.
21398
What is 'Adhobhag' in the context of Panji ranking?
A downward movement in hierarchical status due to marriage into a 
lower-ranked family.
21399
Which ruler is credited with formalising the Panji system for 
Maithil Brahmins in 1326 AD (Saka 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21400
In the context of Panji documentation, what does the term 'Utenrh' 
represent?
The detailed introduction and verification of 32 ancestral lineages 
of a person.
21401
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the paternal side for a marriage to be eligible?
Six generations (marriage is permitted in the seventh).
21402
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the maternal side for a marriage to be eligible?
Five generations.
21403
The Maithil Brahmin community is primarily divided into how 
many 'Gotras' (clans)?
Twenty.
21404
What are the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established through the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
21405
What is the role of a 'Panjikar' in Maithil society?
A designated registrar who maintains genealogical records and 
verifies marriage eligibility.
21406
The Panji system classifies Brahmins who followed the Yajurveda 
(Madhyandina branch) as _____.
Vajasaneyi
21407
The Panji system classifies Brahmins who followed the Samaveda 
(Kauthuma branch) as _____.
Chandoga
21408
Which historical classification groups Maithil Brahmins with 
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, and Saraswat Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda.
21409
What geographic feature traditionally divides the 'Pancha Gauda' 
Brahmins from the 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
21410
In the Panji system, what is a 'Moola'?
The original ancestral village or primary lineage root of a family.
21411
How many primary 'Moolas' were identified during the initial 
formalisation of the Panji system?
167
21412
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Vatsa' Gotra and 'Savarna' 
Gotra in the Panji system?
They share common ancestors, making them functionally the same 
lineage for marriage purposes.
21413
In genealogical terms, who is the 'Beeji Purusha'?
The original progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific lineage or 
'Moola'.
21414
Which script was traditionally used to record the Panji genealogical 
trees on palm leaves?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
21415
The 'Sakha Panji', which documents branch lineages in greater 
detail, was introduced during the reign of which Maharaja?
Maharaja Madhav Singh (c. 1760 AD).
21416
What is the 'Dushana Panji' used to record?
Lineage flaws, defects, or instances where ancestral purity was 
compromised.
21417
What is the 'Chadhvitha' in the process of marriage eligibility?
The identification of 16 specific ancestral nodes (nodes of 6th-
degree relations) to ensure no blood overlap.
21418
Which Maithil scholar and king's authority is cited for permitting 
marriage in the 7th generation on the paternal side?
M.M. Maharaja Mahesh Thakur.
21419
The followers of Samaveda (Chandogas) divide their marriage 
ceremony into which two distinct parts?
Purva Vivaha and Uttara Vivaha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21420
What is the significance of the term 'Svajana' in the context of 
Mithila's matrimonial laws?
It refers to a person belonging to the same prohibited bloodline, 
making marriage illegal.
21421
Who was the first Panjikar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the records?
Gunakar Jha (of Pandua Moola).
21422
Maithil Brahmins who did not enter the formal Panji system during 
its inception were historically termed _____.
Jaibar
21423
Identify the Gotra: This group includes the lineages of 'Dighve', 
'Sarisab', and 'Mahua'.
Sandilya.
21424
Identify the Gotra: This group includes the lineages of 'Pali', 
'Hariamb', and 'Takarwal'.
Vatsa.
21425
Which ancient philosopher first mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' 
(collective genealogical records) in his work 'Tantra Vartika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.
21426
The Panji Prabandh prohibits marriage with the offspring of which 
specific maternal relative?
The mother's brother (Maternal uncle).
21427
What is the primary difference in 'Samskaras' (rites of passage) 
between Vajasaneyi and Chandoga Brahmins regarding the Veda?
Vajasaneyis include 'Vedarambha' (beginning of Veda study), while 
Chandogas do not.
21428
How many 'Pravaras' are associated with the 'Sandilya' Gotra?
Three (Tri-pravara).
21429
How many 'Pravaras' are associated with the 'Vatsa' Gotra?
Five (Pancha-pravara).
21430
The 'Pancha-pravara' of the Vatsa Gotra includes Aurva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____.
Apnuvana
21431
The term 'Paanti' in the Maithil social hierarchy refers to a family's 
_____.
Social rank or status based on ancestral purity.
21432
In the Panji system, what happens to the 'Paanti' of a family if they 
marry into a lower-ranked lineage?
The status is downgraded (Adhogami).
21433
According to the text, which year marked the formal start of the 
'Panji Prabandh' record-keeping?
1326 AD.
21434
Which document is preserved to ensure a lineage is protected from 
the 'Dushana' (defects) of past generations?
Dushana Panji.
21435
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a comprehensive list of every Gotra and 
its associated _____.
Ancient Moolas (Original villages).
21436
M.M. Trivenibhatta (c. 400 AD) is identified as the 'Beeji Purusha' 
of which major lineage?
Mandaur Moola.
21437
Which famous saint's guru, Ramapati Upadhyaya, is documented in 
the Panji records as belonging to the Karmahe-Tarauni Moola?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
21438
To preserve palm-leaf Panji books, they should be dried when the 
sun is in which zodiac sign?
Leo (Simha Rashi), usually between mid-August and mid-
September.
21439
The 'Utenrh Panji' focuses on identifying the names of _____ 
generations of ancestors.
Six
21440
Which Moola is found across multiple Gotras including Sandilya, 
Vatsa, and Kashyapa?
Brahmapura.
21441
How does the text define 'Sapinda' in the context of marriage?
Relations sharing the same 'pinda' or body/ancestry within 
prohibited degrees.
21442
What is the traditional material used for writing the 'Panji' before 
the advent of paper?
Talapatra (Palm leaves).
21443
Which Maithil king initiated the 'Sakha Panji' to reward or classify 
families based on their conduct and scholarship?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21444
The phrase 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat Raksha' in Panji preservation 
translates to 'Protect from _____ and _____'.
Water and Oil.
21445
In which century was the current form of the Panji Prabandh 
(genealogical records) initiated in Mithila?
The 14th Century (specifically 1326 AD).
21446
Which king is credited with the formalisation of the Panji Prabandh 
in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21447
What is the primary objective of maintaining the Panji records for 
Maithil Brahmins?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhata' (caste purity) and verify marriage 
eligibility.
21448
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Udhed' signify in the 
context of a person's identity?
The detailed introduction and lineage of 32 ancestors.
21449
How many generations on the paternal side must typically be 
avoided to consider a marriage valid under Panji rules?
Seven generations.
21450
How many generations on the maternal side must typically be 
avoided to consider a marriage valid under Panji rules?
Five generations.
21451
According to the Panji system, what are the three main categories 
of Maithil Brahmins?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
21452
What status is given to Brahmins who are not registered in the Panji 
records?
Jaywar.
21453
What are the seven most common and well-known Gotras among 
Maithil Brahmins?
Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashar, Bharadwaj, and 
Katyayan.
21454
What is the function of a 'Panjikar' in Maithil society?
A specialised genealogist who maintains records and verifies 
marriage compatibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21455
What is the 'Beeji Purush' in a Panji record?
The earliest known common ancestor or progenitor of a specific 
lineage/family.
21456
The term 'Moolgram' refers to which piece of genealogical 
information?
The original ancestral village of a particular family line.
21457
How many primary 'Mools' (ancestral roots) were originally 
determined in the Panji system?
167
21458
Into which two Vedic branches are Maithil Brahmins primarily 
divided?
Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda) and Chhandogya (Samaveda).
21459
What is the difference between 'Tri-Pravar' and 'Panch-Pravar' 
lineages?
Lineages claiming descent from three or five distinct Vedic sages 
(Rishis).
21460
Who was appointed as the head Panjikar for Maithil Brahmins by 
Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Gunakar Jha.
21461
Which Gotra includes the Mools 'Oini', 'Khouwal', and 'Mandar'?
Kashyap.
21462
The 'Dushana Panji' is used to record what specific type of 
information?
Lineage flaws, defects, or deviations from social/religious norms in 
a family history.
21463
Which historical figure, known as the 'Maithil Poet', has his lineage 
meticulously preserved in the Panji records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
21464
The Panji records identify Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's guru, Ramapati 
Upadhyaya, as belonging to which Mool?
Kariham-Bilaunch.
21465
What is the 'Chhadwaith' rule in Maithil marriage eligibility?
A rule identifying sixteen specific ancestral positions where 
relationship must be checked.
21466
Under Panji rules, can a person marry the child of their mother's 
brother?
No, it is strictly prohibited.
21467
Which king initiated the 'Sakha Panji' during the 18th Century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21468
How many Gotras exist for Karna Kayasthas according to the text?
Only one (Kashyap).
21469
The 'Pancha-Gauda' group includes Maithil, Saraswat, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, and _____ Brahmins.
Utkal.
21470
What defines the geographical boundary between 'Pancha-Gauda' 
and 'Pancha-Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
21471
A marriage in Mithila is considered 'Ashastriya' (unscriptural) if it 
is conducted without _____.
The Siddhanta (Panjikar's approval).
21472
In the context of Mools, what does 'Ayanti Shrestha' represent?
The first or highest category of Mools, such as Khardauria and 
Khauariya.
21473
How many ancestors are traced to define the 16 'Chhadwaith' 
positions for a bride?
Six generations in specific paternal and maternal lines.
21474
What is the primary difference in marriage rituals between 
Samavedi and Yajurvedi Maithils?
Samavedi (Chhandogya) weddings are split into two parts (Purva 
and Uttara), while Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi) are not.
21475
Why is marriage prohibited between the Savarna and Vatsa Gotras 
in some Panji traditions?
Because they share common ancestors (Rishis).
21476
What was the significance of the year Shaka 1248 in the history of 
Panji?
It is the date Maharaja Harisimhadeva formalised the Panji 
management system.
21477
Who was appointed to maintain the Panji for the Kshatriyas of 
Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
21478
Who was appointed to maintain the Panji for the Karna Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
21479
Under which conditions is a marriage allowed even if a 
'Chhadwaith' link is found?
If the link appears on the maternal side of the groom after the 
prohibited generations.
21480
What is the religious consequence of marrying a 'Svajana' 
(prohibited relative) according to the text?
The offspring is traditionally considered 'Chandala' (outcaste).
21481
What does the 'Gotra Panji' record?
The distribution of all Mools (roots) within their respective Gotras.
21482
Identify the 'Beeji Purush' of the Mandar Mool of Kashyap Gotra.
Trilocan Bhatt.
21483
Which Mool is unique for being found in multiple Gotras like 
Sandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap?
Brahmapura.
21484
What is the 'Pari' in the context of Panji ranking?
A system of social status or points based on ancestral pedigree.
21485
How does the Panji document provide historical insight into 
migration?
By recording transitions from 'Moolgram' (original village) to new 
residences.
21486
Which sage is the 'Beeji Purush' for the Sandilya Gotra's Khardaur 
Mool?
Sankarshan.
21487
What is the 'Ghataka' in the Panji system?
A person who facilitates marriage alliances based on Panji 
eligibility.
21488
How did the Panjikars preserve the physical Panji manuscripts?
By drying them in the sun during the lion-sign of the sun (August-
September) and avoiding oil/water.
21489
What does 'Sarpini' or 'Asvajanipari' Panji track?
Prohibited or invalid relationship links that disqualify a marriage.
21490
Which Gotra includes the Rishis 'Atri' and 'Sankriti'?
Vashistha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21491
Define 'Sapinda' as used in Mithila's marriage laws.
Sharing a common body/ancestry within prohibited degrees of 
generation.
21492
Which Mool belongs to the first category (Ayanti Shrestha) and is 
associated with the village 'Sarisab'?
Sarisab Mool.
21493
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the Simhashram Mool?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
21494
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktva Pitritah Saptami Bhajet' 
comes from which scripture?
Manusmriti.
21495
What is the 'Paanti' (ranking) hierarchy for Shrotriya Brahmins?
A four-fold categorisation (Chauvala) of lineages based on their 
purity and status.
21496
Which Mool was added as the 35th main branch later in the history 
of the Panji?
Phandah (branch of Khagram).
21497
What does the Panji record indicate about marriages involving 
'Devadasis'?
The records often mark the maternal side as 'Agyat' (unknown) or 
note the status of the child in society.
21498
The status of 'Jaywar' usually implies what about a person's 
genealogical records?
Their lineage was not originally recorded or maintained by official 
Panjikars.
21499
What is 'Pachhwari Paanti'?
The group or category of Brahmins outside the elite Shrotriya 
hierarchy.
21500
Which sage is associated with the 'Beeji' of the Bharadwaj Gotra?
Bharadwaj, Angiras, and Barhaspatya.
21501
The Panji management system is described as a 'Genome Mapping' 
of which period?
450 AD to 2009 AD.
21502
What are the four 'Samskaras' required for the Samavedi 
(Chhandog) Upanayan?
Namakaran, Chudakaran, Upanayan, and Samavartan.
21503
What extra 'Samskara' is included in the Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi) 
Upanayan compared to the Samavedi?
Vedaarambh.
21504
The 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila Bhatta (7th Century) mentions 
what genealogical practice?
The use of 'Samuha-Lekhya' to protect lineage and caste purity.
21505
Which specific village is mentioned as the source of the 'Mandar' 
Mool's 74 branches?
Mandar village.
21506
In Panji records, what is a 'Daurhit'?
A daughter's son, used to track maternal lineage connections.
21507
How many 'Chhadwaith' positions are checked for the groom before 
marriage?
The groom's 16 positions must be verified against the bride's 
lineage to avoid Sapinda overlap.
21508
What is the primary historical purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' 
system in Mithila?
To ensure caste purity and determine marriage eligibility by 
tracking genealogical records.
21509
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with institutionalising 
the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21510
In what year (AD) was the formal Panji system established 
according to the source material?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
21511
What does the term 'Uderh' represent in the context of Maithil 
genealogy?
A formal introduction of thirty-two ancestral lines required to verify 
marriage eligibility.
21512
The Panji system tracks the lineages of which three social groups in 
Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
21513
According to the Panji rules, how many maternal generations must 
be avoided for a marriage to be considered valid?
Five generations.
21514
How many paternal generations must traditionally be avoided for 
marriage among 'Shrotriya' Maithil Brahmins?
Seven generations.
21515
Define the term 'Panjikar'.
A professional genealogist or registrar responsible for maintaining 
and verifying family lineage records.
21516
What is 'Siddhant' (or 'Asvajana Patra') in the Panji marriage 
process?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
21517
What is the meaning of 'Moola' in the Panji system?
The original village or ancestral root from which a family lineage 
begins.
21518
How many 'Moolas' (ancestral roots) are recorded for Maithil 
Brahmins in the system?
167
21519
Identify the two main Vedic branches followed by Maithil 
Brahmins.
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
21520
Which two Gotras are prohibited from intermarrying because they 
share the same ancestors despite being distinct clans?
Savarna and Vatsa.
21521
Who are the five members of the 'Panch-Gauda' Brahmin group?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithila, and Saraswata.
21522
Which scholar's 7th-century work, 'Tantra Varttika', mentions the 
'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) that preceded the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
21523
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Maithil Brahmins by 
King Harisimhadeva?
Gunakar Jha.
21524
In the 'Shrotriya' category, into how many hierarchical sub-classes 
are the Panjis divided?
32
21525
What is the total number of Panji categories (ranks) for the 'Yogya' 
and 'Panjibadh' classes?
153
21526
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
introduced, leading to further social stratification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21527
According to the Panji rules, why is a person of the same 'Pravara' 
excluded from marriage?
They are considered to share spiritual/ancestral lineage, making 
them siblings in a religious sense.
21528
What is the specific 'Gotra' shared by all members of the Karna 
Kayastha community in Mithila?
Kashyap.
21529
What does the 'Biji Purush' represent in a lineage record?
The earliest known common ancestor or the founder of a specific 
family branch.
21530
In the Panji system, what is 'Dushana'?
A record of social or genealogical 'defects' or deviations within a 
lineage.
21531
What are the four main rituals (Sanskaras) involved in the 
'Upanayana' for Samavedic Brahmins?
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
21532
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurvedic) marriage timing differ from 
the 'Chandoga' (Samavedic) timing?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur day or night, while Chandoga 
marriages involve specific evening rituals after seeing the star 
Arundhati.
21533
Define 'Chadvitha' in the context of marriage verification.
The sixteen specific ancestral positions that must be checked to 
ensure no 'Sapinda' (blood relative) relationship exists.
21534
Which ancient lawgivers' rules (Smritis) form the basis of the Panji 
marriage prohibitions?
Manu and Yajnavalkya.
21535
Marriage with the children of a mother's _____ is strictly prohibited 
in the Panji system.
Brother (maternal uncle).
21536
What happens if a Brahmin marries a 'Swajana' (prohibited relative) 
according to traditional belief?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' (outcast) and excluded 
from Aryan social status.
21537
The 'Panch-Dravida' Brahmins are geographically separated from 
'Panch-Gauda' by which mountain range?
Vindhyachal.
21538
Who were the primary Panjikars appointed for the Kayastha and 
Kshatriya groups, respectively?
Shankardatt (Kayastha) and Vijaydatt (Kshatriya).
21539
What are the three sages associated with the 'Shandilya' Gotra?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
21540
The sages Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagni, and Apnuvana 
belong to which Gotra's Pravara?
Vatsa (or Savarna).
21541
Which specific ancestor is the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandar Moola?
Trivikram Bhatta.
21542
How does the Panji record indicate a marriage that occurred outside 
the caste?
The record marks the lineage as 'Ajnat' (unknown) or lists the 
specific non-Brahmin origin (e.g., 'Dasi').
21543
What is the hierarchical position of 'Shrotriya' Brahmins compared 
to 'Yogya' Brahmins?
Shrotriyas are the highest social tier, followed by Yogyas.
21544
The physical records of the Panji were traditionally written on 
_____ using locally made ink.
Palm leaves (Talapatra).
21545
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya genealogy?
A set of rules or social standing markers used to determine sub-
hierarchies within the Shrotriya group.
21546
According to the Panji, a person in the _____ position relative to a 
girl's ancestors cannot marry her.
Sixth (Chashtha).
21547
List three of the primary seven Gotras that comprise the majority of 
the Maithil Brahmin population.
Shandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap (others: Savarna, Parashar, 
Bharadwaj, Katyayan).
21548
What are the three meanings of 'Gotra' found in the 'Amarkosha'?
Mountain, lineage (Vansha), and name.
21549
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashar?
Parashar.
21550
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Bharadwaj, Angiras, and 
Barhaspatya?
Bharadwaj.
21551
Identify the primary 'Moolas' of the 'Atyanti Shrestha' (First Tier) 
classification.
Khadaure, Khauar, Budhabar, Mandar, Darihar, Ghusaut, Tisaut, 
Karmehe, Nanaure, Babhaniyam, Hariamb, Sarisab, and Sodarpur.
21552
Define 'Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's' genealogical connection to 
Mithila as per the Panji.
His ancestors (Umapati Upadhyay) belonged to the Karmehe-
Nanaure Moola and migrated to Bengal.
21553
Why did the Panji system effectively disappear among Mithila's 
Kshatriyas?
Due to the passage of time and the loss of practitioners who could 
maintain the complex records.
21554
What mnemonic instructions were given to protect Panji books 
from damage?
Protect them from water, oil, and loose binding ('Jalat Rakshet, 
Tailat Rakshet...').
21555
Who was the author of 'Smritisar' whose unintended prohibited 
marriage led to the formalisation of the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
21556
The record-keeping system allows a modern person to trace their 
lineage back at least _____ years.
1000 years.
21557
The 'Panchobhe' and 'Dighway' lineages belong to which 
classification tier?
Madhyam Moola (Middle Roots).
21558
How many main 'Moolas' were initially identified out of the 167 
during the system's inception?
34 (later 35 with the addition of the 'Phandah' branch).
21559
What is the role of the 'Brahmaputra' Moola in Gotra classification?
It is a rare Moola found across multiple Gotras, including 
Shandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap.
21560
Concept: Genome Mapping (Maithil Context)
The metaphorical comparison of the Panji genealogical records to 
modern DNA mapping due to its scientific approach to hereditary 
tracking.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21561
What are the two 'Utsh' (divisions) of Brahmins mentioned based 
on ritual practice?
Chhandogya (Samavedi) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedi).
21562
What was the significance of the year 2000 in the context of the 
Panji source material?
It marked the start of the project to transliterate ancient Panji 
records from Tirhuta script into Devanagari.
21563
In the verification process, what does the Panjikar check regarding 
the 'Matrimaternal' branch?
That the groom is not a descendant of the bride's mother's brother.
21564
Name the scholar who served as the primary oral examiner for the 
'Panji Shastra Martanda' degree.
Mahamahopadhyaya Dr Sir Ganganath Jha.
21565
Which ancient astronomer is listed in the Mandar Moola lineage in 
the Panji records?
Aryabhata (referenced as Aryabhatta in the text).
21566
How are 'Pubaripa' and 'Pachhbaripa' Brahmins distinguished?
Shrotriyas are termed 'Pubaripa' (Easterners) while others are 
'Pachhbaripa' (Westerners).
21567
How many generations are skipped for marriage on the paternal 
side in the modified modern rule for non-Shrotriyas?
Six generations.
21568
Which Gotra includes sages like Mudgalya, Angiras, and 
Barhaspatya?
Mudgalya.
21569
What is the consequence of marrying within the same 'Gotra' 
(Sagotra)?
The marriage is considered invalid as the couple is regarded as 
siblings.
21570
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Jaibar'?
A category for Brahmins who were not formally recorded in the 
initial Panji registers.
21571
In which century was the Panji Prabandh system of genealogy 
management formally established in Mithila?
The $14$th century (specifically $1326$ AD).
21572
Which ruler of the Karnata dynasty is credited with the initiation of 
the Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21573
What was the primary social purpose for establishing the Panji 
Prabandh?
To ensure caste purity ($Jati\ Shuddhi$) and regulate marriage 
eligibility.
21574
The Panji records originally covered which three social groups in 
Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
21575
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udhedh' refer to?
The detailed introduction or genealogical identity of an individual.
21576
How many ancestors' lineages must be introduced in a full 'Udhedh' 
for a Brahmin marriage?
$32$ ancestral lineages.
21577
What is the minimum number of generations that must be avoided 
on the paternal side for a Maithil Brahmin marriage?
$7$ generations.
21578
What is the minimum number of generations that must be avoided 
on the maternal side for a Maithil Brahmin marriage?
$5$ generations.
21579
Maithil Brahmins are classified into which three main hierarchical 
categories?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
21580
What term is used for those who were not recorded in the Panji 
registers during the initial classification?
Jaibar.
21581
How many 'Gotras' are formally recognised among Maithil 
Brahmins according to the source material?
$20$ Gotras.
21582
How many 'Mulas' (original root families) were identified at the 
inception of the Panji system?
$167$ Mulas.
21583
What is the specific term for the founding ancestor of a family 
lineage in the Panji system?
Biji Purusha.
21584
What is the meaning of 'Mula Grama' in the genealogical records?
The ancestral village or place of origin of a specific lineage.
21585
Who was the first designated genealogist (Panjikar) appointed by 
King Harisimhadeva for the Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
21586
Which individual was appointed to manage the Panji records for the 
Karna Kayastha community?
Shankaradatta.
21587
Which person was initially appointed to manage the records for the 
Kshatriya community?
Vijayadatta.
21588
Concept: Siddhanta Patra (Asvajanapatra)
Definition: A certificate of non-relationship issued by a Panjikar to 
authorise a marriage.
21589
The high-ranking 'Ayanti Shrestha' Mulas are divided into how 
many first-class lineages?
$13$ lineages (including Khadaure, Khauaad, and Sodarpuriye).
21590
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara' 
families?
Tri-pravara names $3$ sage ancestors, while Pancha-pravara names 
$5$ sage ancestors in Vedic rituals.
21591
Which three Gotras are categorized as 'Pancha-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj.
21592
According to the Panji system, what happens if a marriage occurs 
within the same Gotra?
The offspring are traditionally considered 'Chandala' (outcastes) 
from the Aryan dignity.
21593
What is the title given to Yajurvedic Brahmins who follow the 
Madhyandina branch?
Vajasaneyi.
21594
What is the title given to Samavedic Brahmins who follow the 
Kauthuma branch?
Chhandoga.
21595
What distinguishes the Samavedic (Chhandoga) marriage from the 
Yajurvedic (Vajasaneyi) one?
The Samavedic marriage is divided into 'Purva' and 'Uttara' (after 
sunset) rituals.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21596
Which Maithil Brahmin Gotra has the highest number of branches 
($74$)?
Kashyapa.
21597
What is the unique 'Gotra' characteristic of the Karna Kayastha 
community in Mithila?
They all belong to a single Gotra: Kashyapa.
21598
Under which $18$th-century ruler did 'Sakha Panji' (branched 
records) and formal hierarchical divisions begin?
Maharaja Madhava Singh.
21599
The 'Chhadwith' refers to how many specific genealogical 
checkpoints used to verify marriage eligibility?
$16$ points.
21600
In the 'Chhadwith' check, what is the 'First Chhadwith'?
The paternal great-great-great-grandfather ($Vriddha\ 
Prapitamaha$) of the bride.
21601
What determines if an ancestor found in the bride's lineage 
prohibits marriage with a groom?
If the ancestor appears within the prohibited degree of the groom's 
paternal side.
21602
What is 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya Brahmins?
The specific family status or 'honour' tracked within the Shrotriya 
branch of the Panji.
21603
How many Shrotriya Panji records are currently recognized?
$32$ Shrotriya Panjis.
21604
The general (non-Shrotriya) Panji records are divided into how 
many categories?
$153$ Panjis.
21605
What is the recommended time of year to dry and preserve Panji 
books to prevent damage?
When the Sun is in the Leo ($Simha$) sign (approx. $16$ August 
to $15$ September).
21606
Which scholar's $7$th-century work 'Tantra Varttika' mentions the 
use of 'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) for lineage?
Kumarila Bhatta.
21607
The Maithil Brahmin community is part of which larger traditional 
grouping of North Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda.
21608
Which specific village is identified as the Mula Grama for the 
'Sodarpuriye' Ayanti Shrestha lineage?
Sodarpur.
21609
The branch 'Khandavala', to which the later Rajas of Darbhanga 
belonged, originates from which Gotra?
Shandilya.
21610
What is the primary script used to write traditional Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
21611
The term 'Panchobhe' belongs to which category of Mulas?
Madhyam Mula (Middle category).
21612
Which sage ancestors are associated with the 'Shandilya' Gotra?
Shandilya, Asita, and Devala.
21613
Which sage ancestors are associated with the 'Kashyapa' Gotra?
Kashyapa, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
21614
The Gotras 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' share the same ancestors; how does 
this affect marriage?
Marriage between them is prohibited as they are considered the 
same lineage.
21615
What are the four 'Samskaras' (rituals) involved in a Chhandoga 
(Samavedic) Upanayana?
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
21616
How do the Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedic) rituals for Upanayana differ 
from the Chhandoga ones?
They include 'VedÄ rambha' (beginning of Veda study) instead of 
just Namakarana.
21617
What is a 'Viji' (or Beeji) in Panji terminology?
The primary progenitor or 'seed' ancestor from whom a specific 
family branch begins.
21618
According to the Panji rules, why is marriage with a maternal 
uncle's daughter prohibited?
Because it violates the rule against marrying offspring of the 
mother's brother ($Nirmata-Bhai$).
21619
What was the historical consequence of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya 
marrying his sister's daughter's daughter?
It led to the formalisation of the Panji system to prevent such 
accidental 'incestuous' marriages.
21620
The Panji system classifies 'Uttal' families. What does this mean?
Families that have migrated or moved from their original 'Mula 
Grama'.
21621
How many main 'Mulas' are considered the primary roots from 
which the $167$ branches spread?
$34$ (or $35$ including the later Phandah branch).
21622
Which Gotra does the 'Brahmapura' Mula belong to?
It is an exception that can belong to Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, 
Alambukaksha, Gautama, or Bharadwaj.
21623
What is the 'Panji Mahtand' or 'Lbdha Dhauri'?
High titles or degrees of excellence awarded to expert Panjikars 
(genealogists).
21624
How did the migration of Brahmins to different regions affect their 
identity prior to the Panji system?
They adopted regional identities based on climate, language, and 
culture, leading to the $10$ Brahmin divisions (Utkala, Maithil, 
etc.).
21625
In the Panji terminology, what is 'Paat'?
A genealogical record or 'leaf' detailing family trees, typically 
around $500$ years old in its current form.
21626
Which branch of Brahmins is known as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
21627
Which branch of Brahmins is known as 'Pubari-paat'?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
21628
Who is the famous Maithili poet whose ancestry is preserved in the 
Panji records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
21629
What is the 'Gutra Panji'?
A register that lists all recognized Gotras and their associated 
original Mulas.
21630
In the context of Panji preservation, what does 'Jalat Rakshe, Tailat 
Rakshe' mean?
Protect the books from water and oil.
21631
Which community's Panji records are currently extinct or no longer 
maintained according to the text?
The Kshatriya (Rajput) community.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21632
How did the Panjikar confirm that a bride and groom were not 
'Svajana' (related)?
By examining the 'Udhedh' of both parties and checking for 
overlaps in the prohibited degrees of lineage.
21633
What is the significance of the year $1248$ Shaka in the source 
material?
It is the traditional date for the 'Panji Prabandha' (management) start 
in Mithila.
21634
Which Mula does the famous scholar Ayiachi Mishra belong to?
Sodarpur.
21635
The 'Tirhuta' script is described as the 'soul' of which system in the 
text?
The Panji Prabandh.
21636
What are 'Chhadwith' points $9$ through $12$ primarily concerned 
with?
The lineages of the bride's maternal grandfather ($Matamaha$).
21637
What was the primary objective of creating the 'Panji Prabandha' in 
Mithila during the medieval period?
To ensure caste purity and verify eligibility for marriages among 
specific communities.
21638
Which three social groups were originally included in the Mithila 
Panji records?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
21639
In the context of Maithil genealogy, what is 'Utaidh'?
A detailed ancestral introduction covering seven generations of a 
candidate's lineage.
21640
How many distinct family lineages (Mulas) are traditionally 
required to be identified for a complete 'Utaidh'?
$32$.
21641
Maithil Brahmins who were not registered in the formal Panji 
system are known by what name?
Jaybar.
21642
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
excluded for marriage on the mother's side?
Five generations.
21643
According to the Shrotriya rules, how many generations must 
typically be excluded for marriage on the father's side?
Seven generations.
21644
What are the two primary Vedic branches followed by the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
The Shukla Yajurveda and the Samaveda.
21645
What is the specific name for Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda?
Vajasaneyi.
21646
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthuma branch of the 
Samaveda are referred to as _____.
Chandog.
21647
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are officially recognised in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji system?
$20$.
21648
What is the total number of original root villages ('Mulas') recorded 
for Maithil Brahmins?
$167$.
21649
Of the original $167$ root villages, how many were identified as 
the 'primary' (Mukhya) Mulas?
$34$ or $35$.
21650
Lineages identified as 'Tri-pravar' claim descent from how many 
hymn-composing ancestral sages?
Three.
21651
Lineages identified as 'Panch-pravar' claim descent from how many 
hymn-composing ancestral sages?
Five.
21652
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being separate clans?
Because they share the same set of five ancestral sages (Pravaras).
21653
According to traditional Indian geography, into which two broad 
categories are Brahmins divided based on the Vindhyachal 
mountains?
Panch Gaur (Northern) and Panch Dravid (Southern).
21654
List the five Brahmin groups that constitute the 'Panch Gaur'.
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
21655
List the five Brahmin groups that constitute the 'Panch Dravid'.
Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra.
21656
Mithila was historically the capital region of which ancient 
kingdom?
The Videha dynasty.
21657
How does the 'Chandog' (Samavedi) marriage ritual differ from the 
'Vajasaneyi' regarding the timing of the ceremony?
Chandog marriages are performed in two parts, with the second part 
(Uttar Vivah) occurring after sunset.
21658
What was the significance of 'Samuha Lekhya' mentioned in early 
texts like the Tantra Vartika?
They were collective written records used to preserve family lineage 
and prevent the loss of ancestral history.
21659
Which philosopher wrote the 'Tantra Vartika', providing the earliest 
known reference to maintaining collective genealogical records?
Kumarila Bhatta.
21660
Which specific scholar's accidental marriage to a close cousin led to 
the formal institutionalisation of the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
21661
In what year of the Common Era was the Maithil Panji system 
formally organised by the state?
$1326$ AD.
21662
In what Saka year was the Mithila Panji system formally 
established?
$1248$ Saka.
21663
Which Karnat dynasty king is credited with the systematic 
organisation and state recognition of the Panji system?
King Harisimhadeva.
21664
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' (also called 'Asvajan Patra') issued by a 
Panjikar?
A formal certificate confirming that the bride and groom are not 
related within prohibited degrees.
21665
Who is the 'Panjikar'?
A state-appointed genealogist responsible for maintaining records 
and verifying marriage eligibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21666
What does the term 'Biji Purush' signify in Maithil genealogy?
The earliest known common ancestor from whom a specific family 
lineage or branch originates.
21667
The Amarkosha dictionary defines the term 'Gotra' using which 
three meanings?
Mountain, lineage (Vansha), and name.
21668
Which ancient text provides the first recorded use of the word 
'Gotra' to mean a family or clan?
The Chandogya Upanishad.
21669
The term 'Pravar' is synonymous with which other Sanskrit term 
meaning 'relating to a sage'?
Arsheya.
21670
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandai Mula, whose lineage was 
documented by the Panjikar Modanand Jha?
Trivikram Bhatta.
21671
How many hierarchical ranks (Paantis) are defined for the Shrotriya 
category of Maithil Brahmins?
Eight.
21672
How many Paantis (Panjis) are traditionally maintained for the 
'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddha' categories combined?
$153$.
21673
The Shrotriya Panji records are often referred to as _____.
Laukik.
21674
Which $14$th-century work by Jyotirishwar Thakur describes King 
Harisimhadeva and the social structure of Mithila?
Varna Ratnakar.
21675
What is the only 'Gotra' found among the Karna Kayasthas of 
Mithila according to the Panji system?
Kashyap.
21676
What specific name is given to the 'Shrotriya' subgroup in social 
classification?
Pubaripaati.
21677
What name is given to Maithil Brahmins belonging to ranks below 
Shrotriya?
Pachhbaripaati.
21678
A traditional mnemonic instructs that Panji records must be dried 
during which solar month to ensure preservation?
The month of Bhadra (when the Sun is in Leo).
21679
What are the three main elements Panji books must be protected 
from, according to tradition?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
21680
In the process of verifying marriage eligibility, how many 
'Chhadwit' (prohibited relationship points) are checked?
$16$.
21681
Marriage is prohibited if either candidate falls in the _____ position 
from any of the $16$ 'Chhadwit' ancestors.
Sixth.
21682
In what traditional script are almost all historical Panji records 
written?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
21683
Under the patronage of which Maharaja of Darbhanga was the 
'Shakha Panji' (branch records) introduced?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21684
The term 'Adhogami' in the Panji system refers to what 
phenomenon?
The downward movement of a family's social rank over generations 
due to marriage alliances.
21685
What is the 'Mula Panji'?
A record detailing the original ancestral village and root of a lineage.
21686
Which category of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally considered the 
highest in social rank?
Shrotriya.
21687
Define 'Sapinda' relationship in the context of Mithila's marriage 
laws.
A relationship sharing common ancestral 'particles', usually 
spanning up to seven generations.
21688
Which specific work by Kumarila Bhatta argues that high-born 
individuals preserve their lineage to maintain social and religious 
integrity?
Tantra Vartika.
21689
In the Rigveda, the word 'Gotra' was initially used to refer to a 
group of cattle or a _____.
Cloud.
21690
Which specific ancestor constitutes the 'First Chhadwit' (prohibited 
ancestor point) for a bride?
The girl's great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
21691
What is the purpose of the 'Sakha Panji'?
To track the expansion of lineages into various branches and sub-
branches across different villages.
21692
How many generations did Maharaja Mahesh Thakur specify 
should be excluded on the father's side for a marriage to be valid?
Seven generations.
21693
The modern study of Panji records is increasingly compared to 
which scientific field?
Genomic mapping.
21694
What was the primary purpose of establishing the 'Panji Prabandha' 
in medieval Mithila?
To maintain caste purity and regulate marriage eligibility among 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
21695
Which historical ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the 
formalisation of the Panji system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21696
In the context of Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Uteá¸ h' 
represent?
The detailed introduction of ancestors (32 lineages) required to 
determine marriage eligibility.
21697
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the father's side for a 'Panjibaddh' 
individual?
Six generations.
21698
How many generations must be avoided on the mother's side for 
marriage eligibility in the Maithil Brahmin community?
Five generations.
21699
What name is given to Brahmins who were not registered in the 
formal Panji records?
Jaywar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21700
Who was appointed as the first registrar (Panjikar) for the Karna 
Kayastha community?
Shankaradatta.
21701
For the Kshatriya community in Mithila, who was the first 
designated Panjikar?
Vijayadatta.
21702
The total number of 'Gotras' identified within the Maithil Brahmin 
community is _____.
20
21703
In the Panji Prabandha, what are 'Mulas'?
The original village or ancestral home associated with a specific 
clan.
21704
How many 'Mula-grams' (original villages) were initially identified 
in the genealogical records?
167
21705
Out of the 167 Mulas, how many are considered the primary or 
'main' Mulas?
34 (later 35 including the Phidha Mula branch).
21706
A Gotra whose ancestors composed five Rigvedic Suktas is 
categorised as _____.
Panch-pravara
21707
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being separate?
They share the same five ancestral sages (Aurva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana).
21708
What is a 'Siddhanta-patra' (also known as Aswajana-patra)?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
21709
The term used for the founding ancestor of a specific Mula in the 
Panji records is _____.
Beeji Purush
21710
Into which three hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided during the Panji Prabandha?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
21711
What is the primary function of a 'Dosha Panji'?
To record flaws or 'kshatran' (degradation of status) within a 
specific family lineage.
21712
Which type of Panji record contains details regarding education, 
titles, and maternal lineages?
Sakha Panji.
21713
Under whose reign was the 'Sakha Panji' (branch records) and the 
Shrotriya subdivision formally introduced?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
21714
Term: Chhaidvath
Definition: The 16 specific generational points or relationships 
checked to ensure no 'Sapinda' (blood relation) overlaps exist before 
marriage.
21715
In the Chhaidvath calculation, who represents the 'First Chhaidvath' 
for a girl?
Her paternal great-great-great grandfather (Vriddha-Prapitamaha).
21716
What is the marriage rule regarding the 'Mother's brother's' children? Marriage is strictly prohibited with the offspring of the mother's 
brother.
21717
Which script is traditionally used to maintain the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshara).
21718
The genealogical records trace some lineages as far back as _____ 
AD.
450
21719
What is the 'Shrotriya' requirement for paternal generational 
avoidance in marriage?
Seven generations.
21720
How does the Panji record distinguish between different branches 
of the same Mula?
By adding the name of the specific village where that branch settled 
(e.g., Mandar-Shai-Mancchi).
21721
Which famous mathematician's lineage is recorded in the Mandar 
Mula of the Panji?
Aryabhatta.
21722
According to the Panji, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was the disciple of 
which Maithil scholar?
Ilamapati Upadhyay (Katih Mula).
21723
What is the significance of the 'Sapinda' rule in Mithila marriage 
customs?
It prohibits marriage between individuals sharing a common 
ancestor within a specific number of generations.
21724
Which historical source text first mentioned the 'Samuha Lekhya' 
(collective records) used by Brahmins to protect their lineage?
Kumarila Bhatta's 'Tantra Vartika' (7th Century AD).
21725
In the context of the Panji system, what are the 'Pancha Gauda' 
Brahmin divisions?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
21726
Which geographical feature serves as the boundary between the 
Pancha Gauda and Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
21727
The majority of Maithil Brahmins follow which two Vedas?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
21728
What is the term for followers of the Madhyandina branch of the 
Yajurveda in Mithila?
Vajasaneyi.
21729
Followers of the Samaveda Kauthuma branch in Mithila are known 
as _____.
Chandoga
21730
Which religious authorities define the prohibited degrees of 
marriage (*Svapakshe*) used in the Panji?
Manu and Yajnavalkya Smritis.
21731
Concept: Mulagram
Definition: The original place of residence of the 'Beeji Purush' 
(founder) at the time of the Panji's formalisation.
21732
How many Gotras are considered highly organised and populous 
within the Maithil Brahmin community?
Seven (Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, 
Bharadwaja, and Katyayana).
21733
What does 'Siddhanta' signify in the Panji marriage process?
The formal public declaration and authentication of marriage 
eligibility by the Panjikar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
21734
What was the 'Svapakshe' scandal involving Pandit Hari Nath 
Upadhyay?
He unknowingly married his own niece due to a lack of 
genealogical records, prompting the formalisation of the Panji 
system.
21735
Which Panji type specifically deals with the verification of Sapinda 
relationships for 6 generations?
Uteá¸ h Panji.
21736
What is the 'Laukika' in the Shrotriya Panji system?
The specific social standing or 'status' of a family branch based on 
marriage alliances.
21737
In the Panji hierarchy, who are the 'Yogya' Brahmins?
The second tier of Brahmins, below Shrotriyas but above the basic 
Panjibaddh group.
21738
Which Gotra is the only one found among the Karna Kayasthas, 
according to the text?
Kashyapa.
21739
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin groups include Karnataka, Gurjara, 
Tailanga, Dravida, and _____.
Maharashtra
21740
What is the primary rule for the 'Beeji Purush' identification?
He must be the earliest known common ancestor from whom the 
Mula branches originate.
21741
In the context of marriage, what does 'Asvajanatva' mean?
The state of not being related within the prohibited degrees (non-
cognate).
21742
Which Panji record focuses on individuals from the 'Pachhwari' 
(Western) region?
Vatnam (Pachhwari Paanth).
21743
What is 'Abhuta-purva' in the context of the Sakha Panji?
It refers to the unique and unprecedented detail of records spanning 
over 1,000 years, found only in Mithila.
21744
Which Mula is associated with the scholar Modanand Jha, who 
received the title 'Panji Shastra Martand'?
Moi-Mohi (derived from Mandar).
21745
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the Mandar Mula?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivitram Bhatta.
21746
The historical document 'Varna Ratnakara' mentions how many 
Rajput clans in Mithila?
72 (with 64 specifically described).
21747
What is the status of the Panji system among Rajputs today?
It has largely become extinct, though families still avoid marriage 
within the same village or maternal clan.
21748
Which month is traditionally recommended to dry and maintain the 
Panji books to prevent damage?
Bhadrapada (when the Sun is in Leo, roughly August-September).
21749
According to the Panji, what happens if an individual marries 
within their own Gotra?
The offspring is traditionally considered 'Chandala' (outcaste) and 
the marriage is invalid.
21750
What is the 'Ayanthi Shrestha' category in the Panji?
The first or highest grade of the main Mulas, including Kharaure, 
Khauware, and Budhabare.
21751
Which Gotra includes the Mula 'Panchobhe'?
Savarna.
21752
The Bharadwaja Gotra includes which famous Mula?
Ekahara (also known as Belaunche).
21753
What is the specific rule for marriage eligibility regarding the 
mother's sister's children?
Marriage is prohibited with the children of the mother's sister 
(*Kathamama*).
21754
The 'Panji Prabandha' is described as a 'Genome Mapping' system 
because it tracks the inheritance of _____.
Genetic traits and lineages (chromosomes) via ancestral records.
21755
What is the historical time frame covered by the 'Panji' genealogical 
records according to the source material?
450 AD to 2009 AD.
21756
In the context of Mithila's genealogy, what does 'Panji Prabandh' 
primarily refer to?
A system of genealogical record-keeping established to maintain the 
purity of castes during marriage.
21757
Which Maithil ruler is credited with formally initiating the Panji 
system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
21758
What specific incident involving Pandit Harinath Upadhyay led to 
the formalisation of the Panji records?
He inadvertently married his first cousin (maternal uncle's daughter) 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
21759
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must typically 
be avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
21760
Into which three hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided after the Panji classification?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
21761
What happens to the status of a Brahmin who was not registered 
during the formal Panji census?
They were classified as 'Jaibar'.
21762
