Which Gotra has 'Jatukarna', 'Angiras', and 'Bharadwaja' as its 
Pravar ancestors?
Jatukarna.
22336
What is 'Pari-Panji'?
A record of marital alliances that tracks the exchange between 
different Moolas.
22337
In the context of the Panji, what is the '1248 Shaka' date equivalent 
to in the Common Era?
1326 AD.
22338
The check for 'Chhadwith' points ensures the bride and groom are 
not related through their _____.
Great-great-grandmothers (Vriddha-prapitamahi).
22339
What is the primary reason for 'Panch-Dravida' and 'Panch-Gauda' 
divisions?
Geographic separation by the Vindhya mountains and regional 
cultural influences.
22340
What is the hierarchical relationship between Shrotriya and 
Panjibaddh Brahmins?
Shrotriyas are the highest-ranked, followed by Panjibaddh and 
Yogya.
22341
The Beeji Purusha for the Mandi Moola is _____.
Trivikram Bhatta
22342
Which Gotra is linked to the ancestors Atri, Garga, and Kaushika?
Kaushika.
22343
In which year (Shaka and AD) was the Panji Prabandh system 
officially formalised in Mithila?
It was formalised in 1326 AD, corresponding to Shaka 1248.
22344
Which King of the Karnat dynasty is credited with initiating the 
Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva is the King credited with its initiation.
22345
According to the source, what was the primary purpose of the Panji 
Prabandh?
It was created to maintain caste purity (Jati Shuddhata) for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
22346
What does the term 'Udegh' signify in the Panji system?
It refers to the introduction of seven generations of ancestors 
required to verify marriage eligibility.
22347
How many 'Gotras' are traditionally identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
There are 20 identified Gotras.
22348
What is the meaning of 'Mula Gram' in the context of the Panji 
records?
It signifies the original ancestral village or place of residence for a 
family lineage.
22349
Which scholar was initially appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji?
The scholar Gunakar Jha was appointed for the Brahmin 
community.
22350
Name the scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to maintain 
records for the Karna Kayastha community.
Shankardatta was appointed to manage the Kayastha records.
22351
Which scholar was assigned the task of managing the Panji records 
for the Kshatriya community?
Vijayadatta was assigned the management of the Kshatriya records.
22352
The three hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins created by the 
Panji are Shrotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibaddh.
22353
What term is used for those Brahmins who did not get registered in 
the initial Panji system?
They are referred to as 'Jaywar'.
22354
According to the Shrotriya marriage rules, how many generations 
must be avoided on the paternal side?
Seven generations must be avoided on the paternal side.
22355
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side for a 
valid Shrotriya marriage?
Five generations must be avoided on the maternal side.
22356
In Panji terminology, what is a 'Siddhant Patra'?
It is a certificate of marriage eligibility issued by a Panjikar 
confirming the couple are not 'Svajana' (forbidden relatives).
22357
How many 'Mula' (original roots) were identified in the primary 20 
Gotras of Maithil Brahmins?
There were 167 primary Mulas identified.
22358
What are the four essential sacraments (Samskaras) for a 
'Chhandoga' (Samavedic) Maithil Brahmin?
They are Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
22359
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurvedic) marriage timing differ from 
the Chhandoga tradition?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur during the day or night, whereas 
Chhandoga marriages include a night ritual involving seeing the 
Arundhati star.
22360
The list of 20 Gotras includes Sandilya, Vatsa, Savarna, Kasyapa, 
and _____.
Parashara.
22361
What is the definition of 'Tripravara' in the Maithil Brahmin system? It refers to a lineage whose three ancestors were composers of 
Rigvedic hymns.
22362
Lineages where five ancestors composed Rigvedic hymns are 
categorised as _____.
Panchapravara.
22363
Term: Chhadisth
Definition: The 16 specific generational points or ancestors used to 
determine sapinda (forbidden) relations for marriage.
22364
What is the historical significance of the text 'Tantra Vartika' by 
Kumarila Bhatta to the Panji system?
It contains the earliest mention of 'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) 
used for preserving lineage purity.
22365
The highest category of Mulas, including Khadaurya and 
Khauadya, is known as _____.
Ayanti Shrestha.
22366
What was the specific social consequence for a child born from an 
'unauthorised' (Svajana) marriage?
The child was socially categorised as 'Chandal' and excluded from 
Aryan social status.
